extracted_codes.context: Person exclusion criteria: Any indication at any time of HIV - ICD9: 042-044*

clinical_criteria: Subjects with WBC out of the normal range will be required to have repeated measures at that level
algorithm_summary: This phenotype algorithm identifies genetic variation that predicts white blood cell (WBC) counts and differentials as markers of immune system health. The algorithm includes: 1) Collection of lab results for total WBC count and differential components (neutrophils, bands, lymphocytes, monocytes, eosinophils, basophils), plus platelet count and mean platelet volume; 2) Exclusion criteria removing patients with HIV (ICD-9 codes 042-044*), dialysis history, or splenectomy; 3) Exclusion of lab values drawn during inpatient hospitalization, ER, or urgent care visits; 4) Longitudinal analysis using repeated measures to identify stable WBC patterns not resulting from acute processes; 5) GWAS association analysis adjusted for demographic traits and covariates, with potential stratification by race and extension to multimarker/haplotype analysis and pathway enrichment studies.
